title: Activist short post-performance
question: After an activist short report, does the stock keep falling — and does it matter who published it or how big the target is?
finding: No tradable post-report drift, in any size bucket. Across 96 campaigns (14 publishers, 2020-26) the 21-day path is indistinguishable from a random day in the same names (edge +1.8pp, dependence-adjusted CI spans 0; the mean even runs positive, the wrong sign for a short), and the small-minus-large cap difference is flat (-0.4pp). Net of borrow the short edge is negative at every horizon and ~20% of campaigns squeezed >+20%. The only structure is the publisher, and the forensic-shops 'win' is one Muddy Waters bounce cohort (pull it out and forensic targets fall -10.7%) — real-looking but small-sample and squeeze-driven, so Conditional, not a validated edge.
